creator: rolf.sommerhalder@alumni.ethz.ch
text:
(In reply to Mathieu Arnold from comment #1)

> what does this adds ?

Well, as I tried to summarize in my description, it adds necessary build options so that native PKCS11 mode is built, so that BIND's DNSSEC tools and in-line signing can use HSMs that make a PCKS11 provider available. 
Please see references [1] and [2] for additional details and example of use.

> Also, has this been submitted upstream ?

According to my current understanding, there is nothing to "upstream" to ISC/BIND, or elsewhere. My changes are only using configuration options that BIND had available since 9.10. These changes are local to the FreeBSD port of BIND only, similar to the ports in Fedora "bind-pkcs11", "bind-pkcs11-utils", "bind-pkcs11-libs".

This port is the basis for another port/package in pfSense 2.3.2, which is however "downstream" of FreeBSD 10.3, e.g. pfSense uses ports from FreeBSD.
